title: Developing S&T Research in the Gulf States
summary: A Belfer Center science and technology team traveled to Kuwait in March to take part in a collaborative workshop with the Kuwait Foundation for Advancement of Science (KFAS) on “Science and Technology in the GCC: Building Research Capacity and Vital Linkages.”
body: A Belfer Center science and technology team traveled to Kuwait in March to take part in a collaborative workshop with the Kuwait Foundation for Advancement of Science (KFAS) on “Science and Technology in the GCC: Building Research Capacity and Vital Linkages.” The Center team included Venkatesh Narayanamurti (Benjamin Peirce Research Professor of Technology and Public Policy), Laura Diaz Anadon (Research Associate in the Environment and Natural Resources Program at the Belfer Center and University Lecturer at the Department of Politics and International Studies, Cambridge University), Afreen Siddiqi (Visiting Scholar at the Belfer Center), and Rebecca Stern , former Research Fellow in the Center’s Energy Technology Innovation and Policy research group and graduate student at Harvard’s School for Engineering). The key objectives for the workshop were to explore current challenges in conducting high-quality research in Kuwait and to discuss strategies for creating university-industry linkages. Among the participants from the Middle East were Amani Albedah (Director, Directorate of International Relations and Knowledge Management—KFAS), Khaled Mahdi (General Secretary, Supreme Council for Planning and Development), Salem Al-Hajraf (Executive Director, EBRC, Kuwait Institute for Scientific Research), and Kuwait University Professors Taher Al Sahhaf and Haitham Lababidi . The Harvard team made a series of presentations summarizing key findings of their research studies funded by two successive grants from the Kuwait Program at Harvard Kennedy School. The team examined the trajectory of research in the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) states, emerging trends in national research capacity, international collaborations, and the nature and effectiveness of industry linkages with national universities. They also discussed key findings they have published and highlighted the importance of building research capacity in key areas relevant for national development and public welfare such as energy and environment. A series of presentations were also made by faculty and administrators from institutions in Kuwait. The workshop concluded with a panel discussion on connecting local education and research efforts to national development goals. result.main_content_en: In March, a Belfer Center science and technology team participated in a collaborative workshop in Kuwait with the Kuwait Foundation for Advancement of Science (KFAS) titled 'Science and Technology in the GCC: Building Research Capacity and Vital Linkages.' The workshop aimed to explore challenges in conducting high-quality research in Kuwait and to discuss strategies for fostering university-industry linkages. The Harvard team presented findings from research funded by the Kuwait Program, examining the trajectory of research in GCC states, trends in national research capacity, international collaborations, and the effectiveness of industry-university linkages. They emphasized the importance of building research capacity in key areas like energy and environment for national development. Kuwaiti faculty and administrators also made presentations. The event concluded with a panel discussion on aligning local education and research with national development goals.
result.main_content_zh: 2017年3月，贝尔弗科学与国际事务中心的一个科技团队前往科威特，与科威特科学促进基金会（KFAS）共同举办了一场主题为“海湾合作委员会国家的科学与技术：建设研究能力与重要联系”的协作研讨会。研讨会的主要目标是探讨科威特进行高质量研究当前面临的挑战，并讨论创建大学与产业界联系的策略。哈佛团队展示了由哈佛大学肯尼迪学院科威特项目资助的研究成果，分析了海湾合作委员会国家的研究发展轨迹、国家研究能力的新兴趋势、国际合作情况，以及国内大学与产业界联系的性质与成效。他们强调了在能源、环境等关乎国家发展与公共福利的关键领域建设研究能力的重要性。科威特各机构的教职人员与行政管理人员也进行了系列报告。研讨会最后以一场关于将本地教育及研究工作与国家发展目标相结合的专题讨论结束。
